category_title: Preschoolers with Autism and communication delays who engage in physical aggression to delay transitions
minimal_context: Chris is playing with an iPad and needs to transition to table work.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Chris, it is time to put the iPad away and come to the table for work time.

STUDENT: (Drops to the floor) No.

TUTOR: We have to do our work now. Please stand up.

STUDENT: (Elopes from the activity area, making vocalizations with poor articulation that are difficult to understand.)

TUTOR: Chris, you need to come back and sit at the table.

STUDENT: (Engages in physical aggression by kicking the table leg hard and throwing classroom items across the room to delay the transition.)

options.A: Set a timer, and when it goes off, have Chris turn it off, give the iPad back to the tutor, and transition to the table.
options.B: Use a request for clarification, asking Chris for explanations and elaborations regarding his refusal to transition to the table.
options.C: Respond with enthusiastic verbal praise, expressing pride and encouraging Chris to keep going with the transition to the table.
options.D: Position the tutor physically away from Chris, providing support only intermittently rather than constantly during the transition to the table.
